fundamentals.name: Intuit Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Intuit Inc. provides financial management, payments and capital, compliance, and marketing products and services in the United States. The company operates in four segments: Global Business Solutions, Consumer, Credit Karma, and ProTax. The Global Business Solutions segment provides QuickBooks services, which include financial and business management online services, desktop software, payroll solutions, time tracking, merchant payment processing and bill pay solutions, checking accounts, and financing services for small and mid-market businesses; and Mailchimp, a marketing automation and customer relationship management. This segment also offers QuickBooks online services and desktop software solutions comprising QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Live, QuickBooks Online Advanced, QuickBooks Self-Employed, QuickBooks Solopreneur financial and business management offerings, QuickBooks Online Payroll, QuickBooks Checking, QuickBooks Desktop software subscriptions, and QuickBooks Assisted Payroll. The Consumer segment provides do-it-yourself and assisted TurboTax income tax preparation products and services. The Credit Karma segment offers consumers with a personal finance platform that provides recommendations for credit card, home, auto, and personal loan, and insurance products; online savings and checking accounts; and access to its credit scores and reports, credit and identity monitoring, credit report dispute, credit building tools, and tools. The ProTax segment provides Lacerte, ProSeries, and ProFile desktop tax-preparation software products; and ProConnect Tax Online bill pay tax products, electronic tax filing service, and bank products and related services. It sells products and services through direct sales channels, multichannel shop-and-buy experiences, mobile application stores, and partner and other channels. Intuit Inc. was founded in 1983 and is headquartered in Mountain View, California.

judgment.summary: Intuit demonstrates robust growth, high profitability, and strong free cash flow, trading at a reasonable forward multiple with significant analyst upside.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 1.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-20.
positives: Revenue and earnings growth both exceed 10% YoY, with analyst estimates pointing to continued strong growth.
Operating margin of 47.0% and profit margin of 21.9% indicate exceptional profitability.
Free cash flow of $5.23B TTM supports operations and shareholder returns.
Forward P/E of 11.30x and a PEG ratio of 0.99 suggest the stock is attractively valued relative to its growth prospects.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target significantly above the current price.
negatives: Total debt ($6.90B) is slightly higher than total cash ($6.78B), though manageable given free cash flow.
Quick ratio of 0.62 suggests potential short-term liquidity constraints if inventory is significant.
Beta of 1.03 indicates the stock's volatility is in line with the broader market.

news.paragraph: Intuit's Q3 earnings surpassed expectations driven by consumer segment growth, and the company raised its full-year guidance. This positive financial update comes alongside news that CEO Sasan Goodarzi will reveal "shocking" insights about AI agents, suggesting a significant strategic focus on artificial intelligence. The company's strong performance and forward-looking AI strategy present a compelling narrative ahead of its August earnings report.

headlines.title: Intuit CEO reveals shocking news about AI agents
Intuit's Q3 Earnings Beat on Consumer Growth & Higher Guidance
